A local authority may by ordinance:

(1) require pedestrians to comply strictly with the directions of an official traffic control signal; and

markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 552. Pedestrians
##### § 552.009 — Ordinances Relating to Pedestrians

A local authority may by ordinance:

    (1) require pedestrians to comply strictly with the directions of an official traffic control signal; and

    (2) prohibit pedestrians from crossing a roadway in a business district or a designated highway except in a crosswalk.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

require pedestrians to comply strictly with the directions of an official traffic control signal; and
prohibit pedestrians from crossing a roadway in a business district or a designated highway except in a crosswalk.
